text.title: LB Monitoring Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to apply the lb-monitoring methodology end-to-end.

step.name: deploy-exporter
step.action: HAProxy: deploy `haproxy-exporter` sidecar reading `/run/haproxy/admin.sock`. Nginx: enable `stub_status` + deploy `nginx-prometheus-exporter`. Kubernetes Ingress: enable controller `/metrics` + create `ServiceMonitor`.

step.name: render-alerts
step.action: Render `templates/prometheus-rules.yaml` with the four alerts (BackendDown, HighErrorRate, HighLatency, ConnectionPoolExhausted), each carrying `backend` label and a `for:` ≥ 60 s.
step.decision-gate: If SLO requires sub-minute alerting, document tradeoff (storm risk) before going below 60 s.

step.name: import-dashboard
step.action: Import `templates/grafana-dashboard.json`. Confirm `instance` + `backend` template variables drop down; rows: traffic + error rate + latency + connections + backend health.

step.name: ship-logs
step.action: For HAProxy: Promtail config (`templates/promtail-haproxy.yaml`) tailing `/var/log/haproxy.log` with regex pipeline extracting status / upstream_response_time / request_id; push to Loki. For Nginx: similar with the `combined` log format.

step.name: validate-and-chaos
step.action: Run `validate-lb-monitoring.py --file artefact.json`. Chaos: kill one backend; confirm BackendDown alert fires within 90 s; confirm Grafana panel reflects the change.
